data.title: Ireland Opens GDPR Investigation Into Elon Musk's X Over Grok Deepfakes: What It Means for AI Stocks
data.description:
Ireland's data watchdog has opened a large probe into Elon Musk's social media firm X over AI-made sexual images. The regulator said it will review how user data was used in the creation and posting of "potentially harmful" sexual images by the Grok chatbot.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Grok is built by xAI, Musk's AI firm, and is built into the X feed. The probe will test if X met its core duties under EU data law. That law, known as GDPR, sets strict rules on how firms use and store user data.

Graham Doyle, deputy chief at the Irish Data Protection Commission, said the agency has begun a "large-scale inquiry" into X. He added that the review will look at the firm's "compliance with some of their fundamental obligations under the GDPR."

The case follows media reports that users were able to prompt Grok to make sexual images of real people, in some cases said to include minors. The regulatory body said it had been in touch with X since those reports came out.

Rising Legal Risk for Musk's Tech Empire

This move adds to rising legal risk for Musk's tech group. France raided X's offices this month as part of a wider probe into AI-made abusive content. The UK data office also said it has "serious concerns" about Grok's use of personal data and its "potential to produce harmful sexualised image and video content."

In the meantime, EU officials are also probing xAI under the Digital Services Act, which forces large tech firms to curb illegal and harmful posts.

X has said it removes child abuse material and non-consent nude content. In a post after the French raids, the firm called the claims "baseless" and said it "categorically denies any wrongdoing."

As things stand, the issue seems to be less about content and more about risk. GDPR fines can reach up to 4% of global sales. That can add real cost for firms tied to Musk, even if X itself is private.

Moreover, the case shows that EU leaders are ready to test AI tools that sit inside social feeds. That may shape how other tech firms, such as Meta (META) and Alphabet (GOOGL), build and guard their own AI bots.

We used TipRanks' Comparison Tool to compare notable publicly traded companies that employ chatbots similar to ChatGPT, Grok, and Claude. It's an excellent tool to gain a broader perspective on each stock and the AI industry as a whole.
data.author: Ran Melamed

data.title: This Is Why Gaxosai Stock (GXAI) Rocketed Today
data.description:
Gaxosai (GXAI) stock underwent a massive rally on Tuesday after the artificial intelligence (AI) applications development company announced that it had finalized a deal with BytePlus. BytePlus is the enterprise technology division of ByteDance, which is the Chinese company behind the Seedance AI video generator and TikTok.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Gaxosai revealed that it has reached an agreement with BytePlus for preferred pricing and significant discounts on the company's AI video generation models. This will allow Gaxosai to offer these models to its consumers through Art-Gen, which is its flagship AI image and video creation platform. The company has also been granted early access to new models from ByteDance.

Vadim Mats, CEO of Gaxosai, said, "We are thrilled to deepen our relationship with BytePlus and leverage ByteDance's world-class AI video technology. This partnership enhances our ability to deliver unparalleled creative power to users worldwide while driving stronger economics for our business. It is a significant step toward making Gaxos the go-to AI platform for both creators and enterprises."

Gaxosai Stock Movement Today

Gaxosai stock was up 60% in pre-market trading on Tuesday, following a 1.94% rally on Friday. The shares have decreased 1.97% year-to-date and 35.58% over the past 12 months.

With today's news came heavy trading of GXAI stock, as some 45 million shares changed hands. For comparison, the stock's three-month daily average trading volume is about 427,000 units.
data.author: William White

data.title: QTUM Gains on Semiconductor Boom, But TipRanks AI Analyst Keeps a Neutral Rating
data.description:
The Defiance Quantum ETF (QTUM) is a tech-focused fund that looks to capture long-term growth from AI and next-gen computing. According to TipRanks' AI ETF analyst, QTUM earns a Neutral rating of 64, indicating it is a solid ETF but not a top-tier pick at this time. The AI model also sets a price target of $127, which implies about 8.35% upside from current levels.

At its core, QTUM is driven less by early-stage quantum firms and more by large chip and equipment leaders. The AI analyst notes that its strength comes from strong semiconductor and AI-related holdings like Micron (MU), Lam Research (LRCX), ASM International (ASMIY), and Onto Innovation (ONTO). These companies are tied to the global demand for faster computing and AI hardware, which has supported the fund's strong recent gains.
data.author: Ran Melamed

data.title: U.S. Stock Futures Fall as AI Disruption Fears Continue to Drag Down Tech Stocks
data.description:
U.S. stock futures fell on Tuesday morning, kicking off a holiday-shortened trading week in the red amid continued fears of artificial intelligence (AI) disrupting software and other businesses. Meanwhile, traders await key employment figures, FOMC minutes, and delayed economic reports. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were down 0.79%, 0.38%, and 0.15%, respectively, at 8:36 a.m. EST on February 17.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Last week, all three major indexes closed lower. The S&P 500 and Dow Jones each dropped more than 1%, while the Nasdaq Composite fell over 2%, marking its fifth straight week of losses. Ongoing concerns about AI disrupting software, real estate, and financial services weighed on investor sentiment.

Turning to trending tickers, shares of health tech giant Masimo (MASI) rallied more than 34% in pre-market trading following reports that the company was nearing a deal with Danaher (DHR) to be acquired for about $10 billion in cash. Danaher (DHR) stock declined about 6% on the news. Meanwhile, Norwegian Cruise Line (NCLH) stock jumped 6% following a Wall Street Journal report that Elliott Investment Management has built a more than 10% stake in the company.

Also, Paramount Skydance (PSKY) stock rose about 3% after Netflix (NFLX) granted Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) a seven-day waiver to hold deal talks with Paramount.

data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: XRP News: XRP Price Bounces to $1.50 as Market Structure Bill Progress Sparks New Buying Interest
data.description:
XRP (XRP-USD) is showing signs of strength today as the broader crypto market sees a small bounce. The token managed to cross $1.50 earlier today, marking the second time it has hit that level this month. While it is currently trading around $1.46, XRP is still holding a 1% gain for the day. Much of this upward move is being linked to new progress on the Market Structure Bill, which is helping to clear up rules for digital assets and giving investors more confidence to buy.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Institutional DeFi Push Is on the Horizon

Ripple is also working on a new plan to make XRP a central part of professional finance. The company recently shared a roadmap for "Institutional DeFi" on the XRP Ledger. This plan aims to bring privacy tools, professional lending, and specialized markets to the network in the coming months. Ripple's goal is to turn the ledger into an "end-to-end operating system for real-world finance," which could help major banks and firms use XRP for their daily business.

Analysts See Utility over Retail Hype

Crypto analyst King Jayden noted on X that XRP has jumped about 38% from its lows earlier this month. He believes the current rally is different from past ones because it is driven by real-world use rather than just social media excitement. He shared that "Institutional DeFi was expanding on XRPL with lending, permissioned decentralized exchanges, and privacy-focused upgrades," and added that the current momentum "felt bigger than retail hype."

At the time of writing, XRP is sitting at $1.46.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: Vanguard S&P 500 ETF (VOO) Daily Update, 2/17/2026
data.description:
The Vanguard S&P 500 ETF (VOO), which tracks the S&P 500 Index (SPX), was down 0.55% in Tuesday's pre-market trading as rising worries about AI-driven disruption across major sectors, particularly software, kept investors cautious.

Overall, VOO has lost 1.20% over the past five days but has gained 11.5% over the past year.

data.title: WBD Stock Gains as Paramount Pressures Warner Ahead of Netflix Merger Vote
data.description:
Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) has rejected a new $30 per-share takeover bid from Paramount Skydance (PSKY), but the story is not over. The board is giving Paramount one week to submit what it calls a best-and-final offer, even as it continues to support its merger agreement with Netflix (NFLX).

Meanwhile, after the news broke, WBD shares are up nearly 3% in pre-market trading.

data.description:

data.title: Stock Market News Today, 2/17/26 – U.S. Stock Futures Dip in Holiday-Shortened Week Amid Key Data
data.description:
U.S. stock futures opened lower Tuesday, kicking off a holiday-shortened trading week in the red. Traders await key employment figures, FOMC minutes, and delayed economic reports.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were down 0.56%, 0.20%, and 0.02%, respectively, at 5:30 a.m. EST on February 17.

Last week, all three major indexes closed lower. The S&P 500 and Dow Jones each dropped more than 1%, while the Nasdaq Composite fell over 2%, marking its fifth straight week of losses. Ongoing concerns about AI disrupting software, real estate, and financial services weighed on investor sentiment.

Turning to economic data, delayed reports on the NAHB housing data for November and December, along with the Personal Consumption Expenditures (PCE) index for December (the Fed's preferred inflation gauge), will be released this week. Additionally, data on retail sales, FOMC Minutes, ADP Employment report, and initial jobless claims are due this week.

On the earnings front, results from Palo Alto Networks (PANW), Medtronic (MDT), Booking Holdings (BKNG), Occidental Petroleum (OXY), Walmart (WMT), Alibaba (BABA), Li Auto (LI), and Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) will be reported this week.

Notably, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was down, floating near 4.02%. WTI crude oil futures were trending higher, hovering near $63.27 per barrel as of the last check. Additionally, the Gold Spot U.S. dollar price decreased to nearly $4,928 per ounce on Tuesday.

Elsewhere, European indexes rose on strong corporate earnings, despite the UK's unemployment hitting a five-year high of 5.2% and slowing growth. Traders are expecting at least two interest rate cuts from the Bank of England this year.

Asia-Pacific Markets Traded Mixed

Asia-Pacific markets traded mixed on February 17, with Mainland Chinese and Hong Kong markets remaining closed for the Lunar New Year.

Meanwhile, Japan's Nikkei fell 0.42%, and the Topix decreased 0.68%.

data.author: Sheryl Sheth
